calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-11-2021 Continued To Jul-12-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-28-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. GUNTER MIHAESCU, Esq. of Law Office of Gunter Mihaescu (Tel: 510-969-7550) for Plaintiff Jessy Manuel;
Atty. JASON S. DIGIOIA, Esq. of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara, LLP (Tel: 510-540-4881) for Defendant and Cross-Complainant Janell Guzman (erroneously sued and served as Jay);
Atty. CARLA N. BRAUNSTEIN, Esq. of Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman LLP (Tel: 925-222-3400) for Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. (erroneously sued as Uber) and Rasier-CA, LLC;
Atty. TRUMAN PHAN-QUANG, Esq. of Law Offices of John A. Hauser (Tel: 714-571-0407) for Defendant/Cross-Defendant Monica Louise Burris.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JANELL GUZMAN's Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Guzman's motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication is denied. Defendant fails to maintain its initial burden of production. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 437c(p)(2).)

"The driver of an automobile must manage his vehicle so as not to create an unreasonable risk of harm to others" (California Body & Trailer Mfrs. v. Albrecht (1971) 16 Cal.App.3d Supp. 1, 3.) There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached this duty of care. The evidence shows that defendant pulled the vehicle next to the bike lane-not the curb-and told co-defendant Burris to exit the vehicle. (Exhibit B to DiGioia Decl., [Burris Depo., 14:1-11; 17:5-10; 17:25-19:2]; Exhibit B to Mihaescu Decl., [Guzman Depo., 50:6-17].) A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that defendant breached his duty of care to reasonably manage his vehicle. Unlike California Body, there is no evidence that defendant legally parked his car prior to the accident. (See California Body & Trailer Mfrs., 16 Cal.App.3d Supp. at 4 ["Obviously, the parking by the defendant of his car on the left side of the road was not negligence, for the settled statement plainly sets forth that defendant lawfully parked his car."] In addition, in California Body, the "defendant did not have any conversation with the guest concerning the opening of the door." (Id. at 3.)

Single order signed for both motion and joinder.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
